hawkish_phrases: Monetary policy will need to continue to remain restrictive for sufficiently long until the risks to inflation returning sustainably to the 2% target in the medium term have dissipated further
more persistence in domestic wages and prices, including from second-round effects related to the near-term increase in CPI inflation, this would warrant a relatively tighter monetary policy path
Domestic price and wage pressures are moderating, but remain somewhat elevated
CPI inflation is still projected to rise to around 3¾% in 2025 Q3
there was no presumption that monetary policy was on a pre-set path over the next few meetings
dovish_phrases: Should there be greater or longer-lasting weakness in demand relative to supply, this could push down on inflationary pressures, warranting a less restrictive path of Bank Rate
business survey indicators generally continued to suggest weakness in growth and particularly in employment intentions
Some indicators of employment intentions had deteriorated markedly, to levels consistent with shrinking employment
a gradual and careful approach to the further withdrawal of monetary policy restraint is appropriate
The underlying disinflationary process was expected to continue
summary: The MPC voted 8-1 to hold Bank Rate at 4.5%, with one dissenter preferring a 25bp cut, maintaining a restrictive stance while emphasizing inflation persistence risks and the need for policy to remain restrictive for sufficiently long, despite acknowledging weakness in growth and employment.
